member.name: Jeff Smith
member.synopsis: Jeff Smith is the Labour MP for Manchester Withington , and has been an MP continually since 7 May 2015.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Tickets: Touting
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what estimate she has made of the cost to consumers of ticket touts operating in the UK.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises that UK consumers have been exploited by ticket touts for too long, which is why we have announced new measures to put fans back at the heart of live events and eliminate the scourge of industrial scale ticket touting. We will pu...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Public Service Broadcasting: Streaming
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what steps she is taking to introduce regulation to ensure the (a) prominence of UK public service broadcaster content on YouTube and other major Video Sharing Platforms and (b) distribution of t
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department has held a series of Ministerial roundtables with the public service media providers and video sharing platforms to encourage industry led voluntary partnerships ensuring public service media content is made prominent, and on fair terms. Wh...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Strait of Hormuz
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what steps he is taking to help re-establish freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK is playing a leading role with France to secure the Strait of Hormuz through a multinational military mission of over 40 nations. HMS DRAGON is in the region. I was in Gibraltar the Friday before last, when RFA LYME BAY left, ready to support with ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Defence

writtenQuestions.heading: Religion: Curriculum
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, when she expects to make a decision on whether to include Religious Education within the National Curriculum as part of the current curriculum and assessment review; and whether that decision will be subject to
writtenQuestions.answerText: It has not proved possible to respond to the hon. Member in the time available before Prorogation.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Schools: Collective Worship
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, when the Department plans to publish updated guidance on collective worship in state-funded schools in England; and whether there will be a public consultation as part of that review.
writtenQuestions.answerText: It has not proved possible to respond to the hon. Member in the time available before Prorogation.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Schools: Admissions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, when the next review of the School Admissions Code will take place; and whether the scope of that review will include admissions arrangements for schools with a religious character, including the use of faith-b
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department values the contribution schools with a religious character make to a diverse school system, and it is important faith schools can set admissions criteria that work for their local circumstances.The government set out in the ‘Every Child Ach...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Trastuzumab Deruxtecan
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to make the drug Enhertu is available for people with secondary breast cancer in England.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon. Member to the answer I gave to the Hon. Member for East Grinstead and Uckfield on 13 January 2026 to Question 103810.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Cancer: Health Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what progress has been made on the Cancer Plan commitment to define and count recurrent cancers, starting with metastatic breast cancer in 2026.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The National Disease Registration Service (NDRS) in NHS England collects, curates, quality assures, and analyses data about people with cancer across the whole of England.The NDRS data improvement team is working with National Health Service trusts to pro...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Drugs: Research
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, whether the scheduled January meeting of the cross-government working group on how best to reduce barriers to clinical research with Schedule 1 drugs took place, and whether minutes will be available
writtenQuestions.answerText: A meeting of the cross-government working group on reducing barriers to research with Schedule 1 drugs took place on 23 January. The working group is comprised of officials from the relevant government departments and regulatory bodies. The group’s role i...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Drugs: Research
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, if she will list the meetings scheduled for the working group on any new issues involving research with controlled drugs during the next six months.
writtenQuestions.answerText: On 16 July, the Government responded to recommendations from the Advisory Council on the Misuse of Drugs (‘ACMD’) on how best to reduce barriers to clinical research with Schedule 1 drugs. The Government committed to set up a cross-government working grou...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Drugs: Research
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, how many meetings have been held of the cross-government officials working group on research with controlled drugs.
writtenQuestions.answerText: On 16 July, the Government responded to recommendations from the Advisory Council on the Misuse of Drugs (‘ACMD’) on how best to reduce barriers to clinical research with Schedule 1 drugs. The Government committed to set up a cross-government working grou...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Coeliac Disease
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps her Department is taking to help increase public awareness of coeliac disease.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Diagnostic services, including tests for coeliac disease, are provided in a range of settings, including community diagnostic centres (CDCs). We are expanding diagnostic capacity through the delivery of the CDC programme. NHS England assesses the delivery...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.answerText: Diagnostic services, including tests for coeliac disease, are provided in a range of settings, including community diagnostic centres (CDCs). We are expanding diagnostic capacity through the delivery of the CDC programme. NHS England assesses the delivery...

writtenQuestions.heading: Poverty
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what steps his Department is taking to reduce the number of people in destitution.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to reducing poverty and supporting low-income families. We expect to spend around £306bn through the welfare system in Great Britain in 2024/25 including around £138bn on people of working age and children. From April, working ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions

writtenQuestions.heading: BBC: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, whether he has had discussions with the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport on the Government's BBC funding model review.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government has launched a review of the BBC’s funding model, to ensure it is fair to licence fee payers, sustainable for the long term, and supports the BBC’s vital role in growing our thriving creative industries.This is an important cross-Government...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: BBC: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what criteria her Department uses to determine who will be consulted on the Government’s BBC funding model review; and if she will publish a list of those that have been consulted.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As set out in the Terms of Reference for the BBC Funding Model Review, the review will take evidence from a range of relevant stakeholders as we seek to assess the merits of different options for securing the sustainability of the BBC. The Secretary of St...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: BBC: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, with reference to the BBC Funding Model Review: Terms of Reference, published on 21 March 2024, what steps she plans to take to ensure that the review assesses the potential merits of all options
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government believes that there are challenges around the sustainability of the current licence fee funding model, and is therefore conducting a review of the BBC’s funding model.As the Terms of Reference make clear, the Review is assessing a range of ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Speed Limits: Exemptions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what his planned timetable is for laying the secondary legislation required to commence section 19 of the Road Safety Act 2006.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department for Transport is currently considering how best to take forward implementation of section 19 of the Road Safety Act 2006.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Primodos
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, if she will implement the recommendations in the report by the APPG on Hormone Pregnancy Test entitled Bitter Pill: Primodos - the forgotten thalidomide, published on 27 February 2024.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We remain hugely sympathetic to the families who believe that they have suffered due to the use of Hormone Pregnancy Tests (HPTs). In 2017 an independent Expert Working Group conducted a comprehensive review of the available scientific evidence, and concl...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care
